introSection:
Kumali is a steel inverted roller coaster located in Flamingo Land in Malton, North Yorkshire, England, UK. It is the second of two Suspended Looping Coaster "Shenlin" installations from Vekoma; the first being Snow Mountain Flying Dragon at Happy Valley in Guangdong, China.

Kumali was named after the leader of the lion Pride at Flamingo Land Zoo.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
Kumali opened on 1 April 2006. It is situated where the Bullet previously stood.

For the 2009 season, a silver platform was added to the cobra roll to help evacuation if the ride stalls. It was later painted blue to match the ride's color scheme. Later in the season, the second train was dismantled.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: Kumali is one of three operating Vekoma Suspended Looping Coaster installations in the United Kingdom. Odyssey at Fantasy Island in Ingoldmells, is the largest, and the other, Infusion at Pleasure Beach Blackpool, is the smallest.

sections.title: Theme
sections.content:
Kumali has an African theme and is named after one of the lions located in the park's zoo. It had dedicated voice commentary and a soundtrack composed by a British composer called ElectricStreetPolice.

Kumali has blue track and yellow supports, with stripes of various colors. The train shares this pattern.

sections.title: Layout
sections.content: After going up the tall lift hill, a large, curved drop follows, heading into a vertical loop. A cobra roll over a lake follows, then a zero-g roll and finally a helix leads into the brake run.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: Kumali had a second train, however it was rarely used and was dismantled some time during 2009.

fullText:
Kumali is a steel inverted roller coaster located in Flamingo Land in Malton, North Yorkshire, England, UK. It is the second of two Suspended Looping Coaster "Shenlin" installations from Vekoma; the first being Snow Mountain Flying Dragon at Happy Valley in Guangdong, China.

Kumali was named after the leader of the lion Pride at Flamingo Land Zoo.

Kumali opened on 1 April 2006. It is situated where the Bullet previously stood.

For the 2009 season, a silver platform was added to the cobra roll to help evacuation if the ride stalls. It was later painted blue to match the ride's color scheme. Later in the season, the second train was dismantled.

Kumali is one of three operating Vekoma Suspended Looping Coaster installations in the United Kingdom. Odyssey at Fantasy Island in Ingoldmells, is the largest, and the other, Infusion at Pleasure Beach Blackpool, is the smallest.

Kumali has an African theme and is named after one of the lions located in the park's zoo. It had dedicated voice commentary and a soundtrack composed by a British composer called ElectricStreetPolice.

Kumali has blue track and yellow supports, with stripes of various colors. The train shares this pattern.

After going up the tall lift hill, a large, curved drop follows, heading into a vertical loop. A cobra roll over a lake follows, then a zero-g roll and finally a helix leads into the brake run.

Kumali had a second train, however it was rarely used and was dismantled some time during 2009.
